_meta.purpose: Image-edit pass — TIER BREAK from Light Lancer (Tier 1 top, L3) to Hussar Conscript (Tier 2 entry, L4). Take the L3 composited cavalry lancer and produce the L4 'Hussar Conscript' variant.
history: v1 (2026-05-06): initial author — Hussar Conscript TIER BREAK with 5 adds: (1) iron banding on the spear haft + reinforced socket at the leaf-tip, (2) scale-mail short sleeves on both upper arms shoulder-to-mid-bicep, (3) second steel pauldron on the SPEAR-ARM shoulder matching the L3 rein-arm pauldron — both shoulders now armored, (4) studded leather peytral on horse chest beneath caparison, (5) steel chest-strap rivet plate on rider chest. Tier-break composite uses --dilate 15. Uses spatial-geometry directional language only (SPEAR-SIDE / REIN-SIDE) — no RIGHT/LEFT/anatomical-RIGHT/LEFT/VIEWER-LEFT/VIEWER-RIGHT — per L3 round-3 directional-bias finding (CLAUDE.md § Part 2 § D). The new spear-side pauldron should land correctly because the rein-side shoulder is already occupied by the L3 pauldron and the model has only one open shoulder to place it on.

v2 (2026-05-07): revised per AD FeedbackV0 — REMOVED the L4 'steel chest-strap rivet plate' positive add (per Detail Economy rule — surface ornament with low thumbnail readability alongside existing pauldron rivets and iron banding). REPLACED with the new L4 iron toe-cap strip on each riding boot (per AD 'boot armor split into smaller upgrades, distributed earlier' — boot-armor split locked L4 toe-cap → L6 ankle plates → L8 full sabatons). Visible adds count remains 5: (1) iron banding on spear haft, (2) scale-mail short sleeves both upper arms, (3) second steel pauldron on spear-arm shoulder, (4) studded leather peytral on horse chest, (5) iron toe-cap strip on each boot. Also dropped the L3 knife-sheath references in the preserve block (knife sheath REMOVED at L3 per AD).
v3 (2026-05-07): pipeline update — drop L1_Base scale anchor from image_urls and prompt body. Single-image input (prior-level composited keeper only) per updated pipeline + docs.
v4 (2026-05-07): scrub belt-studs PRESERVE/negative references — L2 IRON STUDS beat dropped per AD detail-economy (drift-corrected pass).

v5 (2026-05-07): drop dense stud grid from L4 peytral (now "few iron rivets at seam edges only"), drop pauldron-seam + toe-cap visible rivets from positive adds, scrub stale chest-strap rivet plate + L2 belt-studs refs, add prophylactic anti-rivet/stud guardrail negative.

v8 (2026-05-07): retone all metal-color language from cool silver-gray to warm silver-gray matching the rendered L1 kettle-helm tone — cool/blueish steel was reading distinctly different from the L1 baseline's warm gray, getting more pronounced at higher levels. Added negative clause locking out cool/blue-gray steel palette.
v10 (2026-05-07): retone metal language from 'warm gray' -> 'neutral gray' (prior pass overshot warm; balanced gray with neither blue nor brown shadow tint matches the L1 baseline most accurately).

v11 (2026-05-07): scrub model-invisible context from prompt body — removed L<n> level-tag references (model is stateless and cannot resolve our level naming), authoring-decision parentheticals (LOCKED/REVISED/REMOVED dates, per AD FeedbackV0, etc.), and cross-level range refs. All persistent decisions remain in _meta.history + the progression plan + CLAUDE.md. See Archer/CLAUDE.md § Part 3 locked decision #27 (drift-corrected: residual parenthetical level-tags also dropped).

image_urls: <<UPLOAD: UnitProgression/Cavalry/out/v1/L3/composite/cavalry_L3_v1_composited.png>> (content reference — apply the upgrades onto this character)
prompt: EDIT GOAL — Apply FIVE visible additive upgrades to the mounted cavalry lancer in the FIRST input image, marking the TIER BREAK from Light Lancer (Tier 1) into Hussar Conscript (Tier 2). The rider already wears the existing veteran-lancer kit: kettle-helm, mustache (no beard), yellow+red split surcoat over brown leather long-sleeves, wide leather waist belt with plain leather belt flanking the brass buckle, a single steel pauldron disc on ONE of the rider's two shoulders (the REIN-SIDE shoulder — the shoulder closest to the horse's head and mane), a leather grip cord wrapped diagonally around the upper third of the wooden spear haft, an iron pommel cap at the wooden spear haft butt, iron banding on the saddle pommel and cantle, an iron-buckled bridle with iron browband on the horse, a small brown leather hip pouch on the SPEAR-SIDE hip (same side of the rider's body as the upright spear), and no knife sheath or dagger at any hip (the existing knife-sheath beat was removed — the existing hip pouch on the spear-side hip is the ONLY belt accessory). The horse is the SOLID chestnut-brown chibi horse (NO white markings) facing the viewer's RIGHT with the red+blue checkered caparison. CRITICAL SPATIAL ANCHOR — the upright wooden SPEAR HAFT is the most prominent vertical line on one side of the rider, with the iron leaf-tip near the top of the canvas. Treat 'spear-side' as the side of the rider's body / canvas where the upright wooden spear haft is visible (the side closer to the horse's tail), and 'rein-side' as the OPPOSITE side where the rein hand grips the reins, the side closest to the horse's head and mane. Throughout this prompt, do NOT use 'right' or 'left' to interpret placement; use SPEAR-SIDE / REIN-SIDE only. (1) IRON BANDING ON THE WOODEN SPEAR HAFT — add three matte neutral dark-gray steel matching the existing kettle-helm tone (balanced gray) iron rings circling the wooden spear haft below the iron leaf-tip socket, plus a reinforced steel cylindrical socket at the seam where the iron leaf-tip meets the wooden haft (visible as a slightly thicker steel band at the very top of the haft just below the leaf-tip). The three rings are evenly spaced along the upper portion of the haft, each approximately three quarters of an inch wide, sitting between the existing leather grip cord wrap (which remains) and the new socket reinforcement. The wooden haft remains warm-brown wood between the rings. The iron pommel cap at the haft butt remains unchanged. The new iron rings + socket reinforcement give the spear an upgraded iron-banded silhouette appropriate to the Hussar tier. (2) SCALE-MAIL SHORT SLEEVES ON BOTH UPPER ARMS — add chunky overlapping rounded scale-shaped plates in neutral silver-gray steel matching the existing kettle-helm tone (balanced gray, neither blue-shadowed nor brown-shadowed) covering both of the rider's UPPER ARMS from the shoulder down to mid-bicep. Each scale-mail sleeve is about three to four inches tall, sitting OVER the existing brown leather long-sleeve under-jacket. The scales are arranged in horizontal rows where each row overlaps the row below by half a scale; each scale is rounded at the bottom edge, painted with a soft top-down highlight on each scale and a warm-brown shadow under the lower edge of each scale. The brown leather under-jacket remains visible from mid-bicep down through the elbow and forearm to the wrist (no scale-mail below mid-bicep). DO NOT use chainmail / fine ring mesh / interlocking metal rings — the body armor at is SCALE MAIL with chunky overlapping rounded plates, identical in style to the existing kettle-helm scale anchor language used in the Cavalry spec. The scale-mail short sleeves go on BOTH UPPER ARMS symmetrically (both the spear-side arm and the rein-side arm). (3) SECOND STEEL PAULDRON DISC ON THE SHOULDER NOT YET ARMORED — at the rider gained ONE steel pauldron on the REIN-SIDE shoulder (still present, unchanged). at this level, ADD a SECOND matching steel pauldron disc on the OTHER shoulder — the SPEAR-SIDE shoulder, the shoulder where the upright wooden spear haft enters the rider's body from above, the shoulder NOT already covered by a pauldron at this level. After this edit the rider has TWO matching steel pauldrons, one on each shoulder. The new spear-side pauldron is the SAME shape and color as the existing rein-side pauldron — single smooth domed disc of neutral silver-gray steel matching the existing kettle-helm tone (balanced gray, neither blue-shadowed nor brown-shadowed), ~5 inches diameter (slightly larger than the existing pauldron disc visible in the input image). Both pauldrons connect to a warm-brown leather strap that crosses under the surcoat between them. SPATIAL DEFINITION — the new pauldron disc sits in the SAME VERTICAL COLUMN OF THE CANVAS AS THE SPEAR HAFT, at the rider's shoulder where the spear haft passes UP toward the iron leaf-tip; the spear haft passes IN FRONT OF the new pauldron from its vantage on its way upward. The new pauldron is on the SAME SIDE OF THE RIDER'S BODY as the spear, on the SAME SIDE OF THE FRAME as the horse's tail and rump. The new pauldron is NOT near the horse's head, NOT near the horse's mane, NOT on the same shoulder as the existing pauldron. After this edit, both shoulders have matching steel pauldron discs — symmetric. (4) STUDDED LEATHER PEYTRAL ON THE HORSE'S CHEST — add a leather chest-plate covering the horse's chest from the base of the neck down to the front of the saddle, sitting BENEATH the existing caparison (which still drapes over the rump and flanks). The peytral is dark warm-brown leather with a small number of dark-iron rivets at the seam edges only (NOT a dense grid of studs across the leather face — "embossed studs.. use clean wider band instead"). The leather face itself stays plain warm-brown. The peytral is wider at the top (where it wraps the base of the horse's neck) and tapers slightly at the bottom (where it meets the saddle girth). The leather edges are visible along the top and sides of the peytral. (5) IRON TOE-CAP STRIP ON EACH RIDING BOOT — add a single matte neutral dark-gray iron matching the existing kettle-helm tone (balanced gray) strip wrapping the FRONT of each riding boot toe. Each strip is approximately 1 inch tall and 2 inches wide. The strip covers ONLY the front toe portion of the boot, leaving the rest of the warm-brown leather body of the boot unchanged (calf, ankle, heel, and instep all stay leather). Both boots gain matching toe-cap strips symmetrically. This is the FIRST boot armor in the chain — ankle plates arrive at and full sabatons at this level (boot-armor split into three smaller adds per the distribution). ===== PRESERVE FROM THE INPUT IMAGE EXACTLY ===== every other element of the FIRST input image must remain identical in shape, color, position, and proportion. THE RIDER FACE: chiseled chibi jawline; confident flat closed-mouth expression; thick dark-brown eyebrows with a slight stern furrow; large warm steel-blue eyes with a single bright catchlight in each iris; prominent slightly-hooked nose; warm peachy-tan skin tone with soft rosy blush; THICK DARK-BROWN HANDLEBAR MUSTACHE covering the upper lip and curving down past the corners of the mouth approximately half an inch past each corner — handlebar-shaped, NOT bushy, NOT walrus, NOT extending into a beard; cheeks and chin CLEAN-SHAVEN with NO beard, NO stubble, NO five o'clock shadow; dark-brown short hair visible only as a thin fringe at the forehead under the front edge of the kettle-helm. THE RIDER HEAD COVERING: the steel kettle-helm — neutral silver-gray steel matching the existing kettle-helm tone (balanced gray, neither blue-shadowed nor brown-shadowed) domed helm with a forward-projecting riveted brim, two visible round iron rivets on each side at the brim seam, gentle sloping forehead curve, brim projecting forward over the eyes, open face. Smooth painted steel — NOT scale, NOT chainmail. THE RIDER TORSO: the sleeveless mustard-yellow and crimson-red split SURCOAT — viewer-LEFT-of-center half mustard-yellow, viewer-RIGHT-of-center half crimson-red, vertical seam down the chest center, simple round neckline, hem reaches the natural waist — visibly DOMINANT on the chest, fully visible. DO NOT cover the surcoat chest with scale mail, chain mail, or any body metal armor at this level — at the surcoat chest remains uncovered; scale-mail is at the SLEEVES only. The wide brown leather waist belt with brass buckle centered at the front. THE RIDER ARMS AND HANDS: the brown leather long-sleeve under-jacket beneath the new scale-mail short sleeves — under-jacket fully covers both forearms from mid-bicep down to the wrist; the new scale-mail short sleeves cover the upper arms shoulder-to-mid-bicep on top of the leather. Both hands bare warm peachy-tan skin (the spear-side hand grips the wooden spear haft at the existing grip cord; the rein-side hand grips the brown leather reins at the horse's withers). No bracers, no vambraces, no gauntlets at this level. THE RIDER LEGS: dark blue / muted steel-blue knee-length trousers slightly loose at the cuff (visible under the saddle skirt on the spear-side); chunky warm-brown leather knee-high riding boots reaching mid-calf, both boots in stirrups, with the new iron toe-cap strip wrapping the front of each boot's toe per Edit Goal item (5). THE WEAPON: the long wooden spear with iron leaf-tip — heavy warm-brown wooden haft running approximately six feet long, neutral gray (matching the existing kettle-helm tone, balanced gray) iron leaf-shaped spearhead at the top, held vertically in the rider's spear-side hand at shoulder height with the spear-tip pointing UP toward the top of the canvas. The iron pommel cap at the haft butt remains unchanged. The leather grip cord wraps the upper third of the haft above the gripping hand, unchanged. The new iron banding rings + socket reinforcement per Edit Goal item (1). THE REINS: the brown leather reins running from the horse's bit back to the rider's rein-side hand at the horse's withers, gripped relaxed. THE HORSE: the SOLID chestnut-brown chibi horse facing the viewer's RIGHT with head and muzzle on the rein-side of the frame and rump and tail on the spear-side of the frame, standing four-square with all four hooves visible at the bottom of the frame, neck arched, ears forward, single dark eye visible on the rein-side of the head with a bright catchlight. THE HORSE IS SOLID CHESTNUT BROWN ACROSS THE ENTIRE BODY — NO WHITE MARKINGS — no white blaze, no white star, no white socks, no white speckling. Dark brown mane along the top of the neck approximately three inches long, dark brown tail extending behind the rump. The horse's pose, anatomy, solid-brown color, mane, and tail all remain identical in shape, color, and position to the input image. THE HORSE TACK: the brown leather saddle with the EXISTING iron banding on pommel and cantle (unchanged); the brown leather bridle with the EXISTING iron browband and iron square buckles on cheekstrap and throatlash (elements, unchanged); the new studded leather peytral on the horse's chest per Edit Goal item (4) sits BENEATH the caparison's chest panel. THE CAPARISON: the large rectangular cloth caparison with red+blue checkered pattern (alternating crimson-red and steel-blue squares) and steel-blue trim band along the lower edge with shallow rectangular tabs — UNCHANGED, identical to the input image, except now its chest panel sits OVER the new studded leather peytral (the caparison still drapes the horse's back, rump, and flanks the same way). THE EXISTING KIT: the single steel pauldron disc on the REIN-SIDE shoulder (unchanged shape and position — at this becomes ONE OF TWO MATCHING DISCS; the SECOND disc is added on the SPEAR-SIDE shoulder per Edit Goal item 3); the leather grip cord wrap on the spear haft; the iron banding on the saddle pommel and cantle; the hip pouch on the spear-side hip (the only belt accessory) — all preserved unchanged in shape, color, and position. NO knife sheath or dagger at any hip (removed at). ===== POSE (must match the input image exactly) ===== Three-quarter view, the rider's body angled approximately fifteen to twenty-five degrees toward the viewer's left so the rider's body presents to camera. The HORSE faces toward the viewer's RIGHT. Rider sits upright in the saddle with both feet in stirrups, slight forward lean. THE SPEAR ARM (the rider's arm on the spear-side of the frame) is bent at the elbow with the upper arm vertical and the forearm angled slightly forward; the hand grips the wooden spear haft at the existing grip cord wrap; the spear stands roughly VERTICAL with the iron leaf-tip pointing UP toward the top of the canvas; the knuckles of the gripping hand visible to camera. THE REIN ARM (the rider's arm on the rein-side of the frame) is extended forward and slightly down, elbow bent at roughly 110 degrees; the hand grips the brown leather reins at the level of the horse's withers / shoulder; the rein hand is visible to camera holding the reins in a relaxed grip. The pose reads as a calm mounted patrol stance. NOT a charge, NOT a couched-lance attack, NOT a rear, NOT a pivot, NOT a walk, NOT a gallop. ===== STYLE / OUTLINE ===== Supercell / Kingshot stylized mobile game art, hand-painted digital illustration with soft painted shading and visible brushwork, warm subsurface skin, saturated rich colors, warm shadow tones never gray, soft painted highlights on metal and leather. Clean thin black silhouette outline around the entire rider+horse+spear+caparison silhouette in deep warm brown-black. Interior linework remains soft and value-driven, never hard black. ===== FRAMING / SCALE (must match these canonical numbers exactly) ===== 1024x1024 square canvas. Rider+horse silhouette occupies approximately 77.6% of canvas vertical height (~795px tall) and approximately 60.6% of canvas horizontal width (~621px wide). TOP of rider's helm at approximately 11.4% from top of canvas (~117px headroom). BOTTOM of horse's hooves at approximately 10.9% from bottom of canvas (~112px below). Rider+horse vertically centered. The wooden spear haft extends UP past the helm with the iron leaf-tip near or at the canvas top edge. DO NOT zoom in, DO NOT shrink, DO NOT add wider borders, DO NOT crop the helm or hooves, DO NOT shrink the spear so its tip falls below the helm, DO NOT shift to bottom edge. Clean white background with no environmental context, minimal contact shadow under the horse's hooves, 1:1 square aspect ratio. ===== NEGATIVE — DO NOT ===== DO NOT remove or shrink or cover the mustard-yellow and crimson-red split surcoat — the chest livery must remain visibly DOMINANT on the chest, fully visible; DO NOT replace the surcoat with a body coat, gambeson, robe, mantle, or armor; DO NOT cover the surcoat chest with scale mail, chain mail, or breastplate at this level — at the scale-mail is on the upper-arm SLEEVES ONLY; the surcoat chest must remain fully visible and dominant; DO NOT add a cuirass, breastplate, plate body armor, body coat, gambeson, or any chest body armor at this level (those arrive at this level+); DO NOT use chainmail / chain mail / fine ring mesh / interlocking metal rings on the new sleeves — they are SCALE MAIL with chunky overlapping rounded plates; DO NOT extend the scale-mail sleeves below mid-bicep — the leather under-jacket must remain visible from mid-bicep down to the wrist; DO NOT add a closed helm, sallet, half-visor, full visor, helm crest, helm horns, or any structural addition to the kettle-helm — the helm stays exactly as the existing kettle-helm; DO NOT remove the kettle-helm or replace it with a different helm (sallet arrives at this level); DO NOT add iron rivets, studs, embossed dots, or any small metal punctuation along the leather waist belt, the leather under-jacket sleeves, the surcoat hem, the saddle leather, the bridle straps, the caparison panels, the rider's hands, or any surface beyond what the input image already shows; DO NOT add metal banding rings or riveted hardware appearing as decoration on the kettle-helm body, the surcoat, or the wooden spear haft middle (the existing kettle's existing brim rivets are functional construction rivets and stay unchanged); DO NOT render any new metal piece in cool blue-gray steel, bluish steel, icy silver, chrome, OR overly warm/brown-tinted steel — every new metal addition must match the NEUTRAL gray tone of the existing kettle-helm and spear leaf-tip (balanced gray, NEITHER blue-shadowed NOR brown-shadowed; soft painted highlights that read clean-white, neither cool nor warm); DO NOT change any facial feature; DO NOT grow the mustache into a beard or add stubble or a five o'clock shadow — cheeks and chin stay clean-shaven with NO beard at all; DO NOT change the surcoat colors, panel layout, neckline, or hem; DO NOT change the trouser color or boot color; DO NOT add full sabatons, ankle plates, greaves, tassets, or any leg metal beyond the new toe-cap strip on each boot at this level — full sabatons arrive at and ankle plates arrive at this level; at only the toe-cap strip on the FRONT of each boot is added; DO NOT add a dagger, knife, knife sheath, dagger sheath, or any second belt accessory — the existing hip pouch is the ONLY belt accessory; DO NOT add a chest-strap rivet plate, baldric, X-strap rivet, or any visible chest-mounted plate at this level; DO NOT add any white markings on the horse — no white blaze, no white star, no white socks, no white speckling; DO NOT swap the hand gripping the spear; DO NOT swap the hand gripping the reins; DO NOT change the spear shape, the wooden haft length, the iron leaf-tip shape, or replace the wooden haft with a steel lance haft (steel haft arrives at this level); DO NOT add a pennon flag or banner to the spear (that arrives at this level); DO NOT add bracers, vambraces, or gauntlets — the forearms remain leather-under-jacket only; DO NOT add a leather X-strap chest harness, baldric, sword belt, or any visible chest strap at this level — no chest-strap rivet plate is added at this level.
